header.title: KeyPing
header.punchline: Detect weak credentials, enforce hygiene and keep your vault fully offline.

health.label: Module Health
health.state: Active development / Stable core / Daily use

overview.title: What KeyPing is
overview.lead: KeyPing is a desktop password manager focused on credential hygiene: detecting weak, reused and risky passwords while keeping all data local and offline.
overview.copy: This is not a concept demo. It is an actively developed product with stable core features, production-level release discipline and security controls designed for real use.

why.title: Why I Built KeyPing
why.subtitle: Why this project exists
why.lead: KeyPing was built to solve real password problems under real pressure, not just to store entries in a vault.

reuse.title: Password reuse
reuse.copy: Reused credentials multiply risk across services. One leaked password can compromise several accounts.

weak.title: Weak passwords
weak.copy: Weak patterns are still common. Users need clear signals, not vague warnings, to improve quickly.

cloud.title: Cloud dependency
cloud.copy: Many managers require cloud trust by default. KeyPing is designed for local-first control.

privacy.title: Privacy under stress
privacy.copy: When incidents happen, privacy and response speed matter at the same time.

focus.title: Critical response feature
focus.copy: Each credential can store a direct password-change URL. In a breach, that one-click path reduces friction and reaction time exactly when stress is highest.

metrics.title: Engineering Proof
metrics.subtitle: Real build signals
metrics.statement: Built as a real desktop product with security boundaries between renderer, preload and main process.

visuals.title: Product in action
visuals.lead: Real product walkthrough: from vault activity and password creation to health diagnostics, generator controls and security settings.

captions.flow: Starts in the password list, opens Create Password, generates a new credential and returns to an updated list with the new password details selected.
captions.dashboard: Security overview with strong/medium/weak distribution, 2FA count, folders, duplicates, recent activity and information section.
captions.list: Operational password list with selected-entry details, letting you inspect records without leaving the vault view.
captions.health: Password Health ring with global score plus strong/medium/weak breakdown, reasons and duplicate-risk visibility.
captions.health2: Extended health panel that complements the main view with additional diagnostics and actionable context.
captions.healthCombo: Complete Password Health view: global score, quality distribution, risk reasons, duplicate detection and extended diagnostics.
captions.generator: Password generator with configurable length and toggles for uppercase, lowercase, numbers and symbols.
captions.settings: Vault protection settings: master password change, inactivity auto-lock timeout, failed attempts and delay policy.
captions.settings2: System settings: language, automatic updates, retained vault versions and encrypted import/export options.

features.title: Core features
items: Folder organization with drag and drop

security.title: Security model
items: Vault encrypted on disk using AES-256-GCM
No mandatory cloud sync and no account required
Clipboard auto-clear only when the copied secret still matches
Escalating cooldown after failed master unlock attempts
Vault structure validation and anomaly/corruption checks

architecture.title: Architecture

bridge.label: IPC bridge
bridge.value: Secure preload API with contextIsolation enabled

vault.label: Vault
vault.value: Encrypted local file managed by the Electron main process

flow: The UI requests operations through preload IPC
The main process validates and executes operations securely
The vault module encrypts and decrypts local storage
The UI receives sanitized metadata and operation state

integrity.title: Release integrity
integrity.copy: Each release includes checksums and detached signature files.
files: SHA256SUMS.txt.asc (detached GPG signature in ASCII-armored format)

roadmap.title: Roadmap
items: Optional breach-check integrations with a privacy-preserving approach
Expanded automated test coverage across IPC and vault logic
Improved UX for import conflict resolution
More accessibility and keyboard navigation polish

header.title: Engineering capabilities
header.lead: How I design systems, solve product constraints and deliver maintainable software.

system.title: System Engineering
system.desc: I design modular software systems with clear boundaries, stable interfaces and long-term maintainability.
points: Modular architecture with explicit domain boundaries
Structural decisions guided by maintainability and scale
Layer integration without coupling business logic to UI details
Codebase organization for predictable evolution over time

desktop.title: Desktop & Product Engineering
desktop.desc: I build desktop products as real production software, not as throwaway demos.
points: Electron applications with real runtime constraints
Offline-first behavior and local reliability by design
End-to-end ownership from interface to packaging
Delivery focused on usable product increments

security.title: Security-Oriented Development
security.desc: I treat security as a system property: boundaries, data protection and predictable trust models.
points: Security controls integrated into architecture decisions
Local encrypted storage and sensitive data minimization
Trust boundary separation across runtime layers
Integrity checks and defensive handling of critical flows

ux.title: Technical UX
ux.desc: I turn technical tools into understandable experiences through structured flows, state visibility and usable interaction design.
points: Complex flows reduced to clear operator actions
State visibility for diagnostics, confidence and control
Interaction patterns designed for speed and precision
Onboarding that reduces friction in technical contexts

ai.title: AI-Assisted Engineering
ai.desc: I use AI as an engineering multiplier for speed, exploration and implementation quality.
points: Rapid exploration of architecture alternatives
Faster prototyping and iteration loops
Assisted refactoring with explicit technical validation
AI integrated as part of the toolchain, not as superficial noise

integration.title: Full Stack / Integration Thinking
integration.desc: I connect frontend, backend and runtime into a coherent product architecture.
points: Frontend, backend and runtime responsibilities aligned
API and application logic designed as one system
Cross-layer problem solving with product context
Implementation decisions tied to operational behavior

capabilities.closing: I build software as an integrated system: architecture, product constraints, security posture, technical UX and delivery execution.
